div.head: The Minister in Switzerland (Wilson) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
293. I have just been informed by an American, in whom I have complete confidence, that he has learned that the propositions presented
to the Italians in the recent tripartite conversations at Paris were not transmitted to Ethiopia at the time but only a few days ago; that the Abyssinian representatives here have today received a reply to the effect that the Negus would agree to these propositions as a basis for discussion under two conditions; (1) that the concessions by Ethiopia would not be made to Italy qua Italy but to the League though Italy could profit under them and, (2) that the Negus as a member of the League would have a say in the appointment of the “administrators”.

So far as my informant is aware the fact[s] of this reply have not yet been communicated to the powers concerned and still remains a secret in the Ethiopian delegation. I am therefore not repeating this telegram anywhere.
